---
title: Media Collection
description: File upload and media asset management
---

## Overview

The Media collection handles file uploads and media asset management for the marketplace, including product images, tenant logos, and other uploaded files.

## Collection Configuration

- **Slug**: `media`
- **Upload**: Enabled (this is an upload collection)

## Fields

<ParamField path="alt" type="text" required>
  Alternative text for the media asset (important for accessibility and SEO).
</ParamField>

### Auto-generated Upload Fields

Payload automatically adds the following fields to upload collections:

<ParamField path="filename" type="text">
  The original filename of the uploaded file.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="mimeType" type="text">
  The MIME type of the uploaded file (e.g., "image/jpeg", "image/png").
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="filesize" type="number">
  Size of the file in bytes.
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="width" type="number">
  Image width in pixels (for image files).
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="height" type="number">
  Image height in pixels (for image files).
</ParamField>

<ParamField path="url" type="text">
  Public URL to access the uploaded file.
</ParamField>

## Relationships

- **Referenced by**:
  - Tenants collection (via image field)
  - Products collection (via image field)

## Access Control

- **Read**: `() => true` (public read access)
- Other operations use default access patterns

## Source Schema

```typescript
import type { CollectionConfig } from 'payload'

export const Media: CollectionConfig = {
  slug: 'media',
  access: {
    read: () => true,
  },
  fields: [
    {
      name: 'alt',
      type: 'text',
      required: true,
    },
  ],
  upload: true,
}
```

## Key Features

- **Upload Enabled**: Full file upload support
- **Public Read**: All media is publicly accessible
- **Accessibility**: Required alt text for images
- **Auto Metadata**: Automatic extraction of file metadata
- **Multi-use**: Referenced by multiple collections

## Usage Patterns

The Media collection is used for:

- **Product Images**: Main product photos and galleries
- **Tenant Branding**: Store logos and branding assets
- **General Uploads**: Any file upload needs in the marketplace

## Storage Considerations

- Files are typically stored in the configured upload directory
- The `url` field provides the public-facing URL
- Image dimensions are automatically calculated and stored
- MIME type validation can be configured in Payload config
